nodes.label: Curriculum Coordination Shaping Rationale
nodes.rationale: Curriculum coordination shaping via shared network-throughput bonus (--net-weight) and downstream-saturation penalty (--coord-penalty) was tested and disabled by default (weight 0.0) as it monotonically degraded real performance vs pure-local waiting-time/pressure policy. The global bonus caused reward inflation without providing learnable credit.

nodes.label: Anti-Thrashing Action Semantics
nodes.rationale: Enforces minimum green time (min_green) and inserts automatic yellow transitions when switching phases, preventing the RL agent from flickering traffic light signals too rapidly.

nodes.label: Max-Pressure Reward Rationale
nodes.rationale: Based on PressLight/MPLight. Minimizing pressure (queue on incoming minus queue on outgoing) stabilizes queue lengths and prevents dumping vehicles into saturated downstream links.

nodes.label: Anti-Starve Reward Rationale
nodes.rationale: Adds a penalty for the worst-starved lane to avoid the agent camping indefinitely on the busiest phase slot where flowing lanes dominate wait times.

nodes.label: Actuated Baseline Heuristic Corrections
nodes.rationale: Corrects legacy mapping of signal indices to unique lanes and uses cumulative waiting time instead of last-step halting count. Halting count drops to near-zero as soon as traffic moves (causing oscillations), whereas waiting time is monotone and serves as a reliable starvation signal.

nodes.label: Light Control via setRedYellowGreenState
nodes.rationale: SUMO switches TLS into a one-phase override program where setPhase(tls, 6) raises errors if an explicit state string is set. Crucial for env function.

nodes.label: Load-bearing TraCI Labels
nodes.rationale: Prevents probe and training sessions from sharing one global TraCI instance.

nodes.label: Policy Collapse under Short Training
nodes.rationale: At 5 episodes, DQN policy collapses to a single green slot and delta stops incrementing. Needs >= 20 episodes.

nodes.label: State/Action Size Mismatch Fallback
nodes.rationale: If checkpoint was trained on different layout, falls back to actuated control with fallback:state_size_mismatch.

nodes.label: ATLAS V1 Architecture Baseline
nodes.rationale: 12 independent Double-DQN agents using BaseTrafficAI MLP (2x128 ReLU), max_pressure_net reward, and local-only state. Wait/vehicle lower by 7%, throughput tied vs native actuated.

nodes.label: ATLAS V2 Target Architecture
nodes.rationale: Upgrades backbone to FRAP phase-invariant encoder, parameter sharing, CoLight GAT communication, MAPPO centralized critic, pure local pressure reward, and detector-noise domain randomization.

nodes.label: FRAP Phase-Invariant Encoder
nodes.rationale: Phase-symmetry-invariant encoder sharing parameters across all 12 lights, achieving 12x sample reuse to mitigate MAPPO sample inefficiency.

nodes.label: CoLight Graph Attention Network Channel
nodes.rationale: Learns attention over upstream, downstream, and cross-street neighbors instead of fixed neighbor summary averages, avoiding local-only ceiling.

nodes.label: MAPPO Centered Training Decentralized Execution
nodes.rationale: Decentralized actors choose actions locally, while a centralized critic uses global state to assign credit, shifting coordination shaping out of the local reward.

nodes.label: Pure Local Pressure Reward Definition
nodes.rationale: Avoids reward-shaping inflation and degradation observed when using naive shared corridor-throughput bonuses.

nodes.label: Detector-Noise Domain Randomization
nodes.rationale: Injects false-negatives (0-15%) and lateral displacement (sigma=0.5m) to bridge the sim-to-real perception gap.

nodes.label: Tiered Fallback Signal Control Mechanism
nodes.rationale: 5-level fallback logic from healthy RL control down to fixed-time TOD plan. Essential for DOT safety requirements; transitions down are immediate, while upgrading requires N minutes of green-status.

nodes.label: Counterfactual Shadow Mode Evaluation
nodes.rationale: RL agent runs in cabinet emitting actions but not actuating. Compares RL action delay vs observed timing via a digital twin to safety-gate live pilot.

nodes.label: Max Pressure Reward Mode
nodes.rationale: Formulated as -|queue_in - queue_out| / n. Literature standard that keeps DQN loss stable (~0.5) compared to differential reward.

nodes.label: Combined Reward Mode (Negative)
nodes.rationale: Combined arrived and wait delta (alpha * arrived - beta * delta_wait). Over-weighted throughput and regressed badly; documented negative result.

nodes.label: Differential Reward Mode
nodes.rationale: Delta wait minus switch penalty. Original reward format, but can suffer from high loss variance (28-130).

nodes.label: Anti-Starve Reward Mode
nodes.rationale: Differential reward minus starve penalty times max lane wait.

nodes.label: Signal Control Baselines
nodes.rationale: Evaluation includes fixed (round-robin), actuated (greedy max-wait with min-green hysteresis), and native_actuated (SUMO's built-in logic).

nodes.label: SUMO Calibration Integrity Status
nodes.rationale: Details what data is real (turn-ratio counts for 6 of 12 intersections, OSM junction matches) vs estimated (remaining turn-ratios, saturation flows using HCM defaults, car-following parameters, passenger-only mix, single-hour demand profile, no ped/bike counts, routeSampler-inferred OD matrix).

nodes.label: Bellevue City Engagement Priorities
nodes.rationale: Critical long poles needed to remove scare quotes from 'calibrated SUMO': P0 (24h counts, timing plans), P1 (saturation flow surveys, heavy vehicle fractions), P2 (peak counts, ped/bike counts), P3 (OD from ITS).

nodes.label: Machine Learning Model Reinfrment Learning Agent
nodes.norm_label: machine learning model reinfrment learning agent

nodes.label: PyTorch
nodes.source_file: PyTorch AI_ Reasons why it's a good choice.pdf
nodes.rationale: Chosen as the deep learning framework for the traffic management research project due to its support for dynamic computational graphs, debugging transparency, and strong research ecosystem, which align with the project's requirements for adaptive model evolution and custom traffic optimization targets.

nodes.label: Dynamic Computational Graph Architecture
nodes.source_file: PyTorch AI_ Reasons why it's a good choice.pdf
nodes.rationale: Employs an imperative, define-by-run approach where graphs are built dynamically during execution. This supports: 1) Adaptive Model Evolution: modifying network structures between training iterations without restarts to handle complex temporal traffic patterns; 2) Conditional Logic Integration: incorporating Python control flow for rush hour vs off-peak scenarios; 3) Iterative Research Cycle: accelerating hypothesis testing by eliminating compile-then-execute overhead.

nodes.label: Debugging and Development Transparency
nodes.source_file: PyTorch AI_ Reasons why it's a good choice.pdf
nodes.rationale: Prioritizes developer intuition and transparency through: 1) Native Python Integration: tensors behave like NumPy arrays to reduce context-switching and integration complexity; 2) Eager execution reporting runtime errors immediately with Python stack traces; 3) Interactive inspection of values, operations, and hypotheses in real-time.

nodes.label: Research and Experimentation Ecosystem
nodes.source_file: PyTorch AI_ Reasons why it's a good choice.pdf
nodes.rationale: Provides alignment with the academic machine learning community, giving direct access to cutting-edge research in time-series forecasting, reinforcement learning, and computer vision. Features modular components (torch.nn) and flexible automatic differentiation (autograd).

nodes.label: torch.nn Module
nodes.source_file: PyTorch AI_ Reasons why it's a good choice.pdf
nodes.rationale: A comprehensive and flexible collection of neural network components that can be easily extended or customized for traffic modeling requirements.

nodes.label: PyTorch Autograd
nodes.source_file: PyTorch AI_ Reasons why it's a good choice.pdf
nodes.rationale: An automatic differentiation system that gives fine-grained control over gradient computation, essential for custom loss functions capturing traffic management optimization targets (e.g. balancing wait time equity vs throughput).

nodes.label: Traffic Management Research
nodes.source_file: PyTorch AI_ Reasons why it's a good choice.pdf
nodes.rationale: The core application domain of the project at the intersection of transportation engineering and machine learning. Involves modeling complex traffic patterns with temporal dependencies, rush-hour scenarios, and optimization goals like wait time equity and total throughput.
